reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect content accuracy, reciting the script exactly as requested without any errors. The fluency and naturalness are high, with a clean, high-quality voice. However, regarding the core paralinguistic instruction—to start smooth and gradually become hoarse—the model fails to comply. The voice remains consistently smooth, clear, and bright throughout the entire utterance (00:00 - 0:12). There is no perceptible shift in timbre towards hoarseness or vocal strain; the vocal texture is static. While the audio is high quality, the complete absence of the requested dynamic timbre adjustment constitutes a major issue in meeting the specific prompt requirements.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails primarily on content accuracy. The script required is 'why can't I sing anymore?', but the model hallucinates a different ending: 'I can't seem to sing any lower' (00:06-00:08). This changes the meaning of the sentence and constitutes a critical failure. Regarding the core requirement, the audio does not demonstrate a transition from smooth to hoarse. Instead, the voice adopts a static, slightly breathy or low-fidelity timbre from the very beginning and maintains it throughout. There is no dynamic adjustment as requested.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. While both models failed the core challenge of dynamically changing the timbre from smooth to hoarse (both produced relatively static timbres), Model 1 successfully generated the correct text with high fluency and naturalness. Model 2, conversely, hallucinated the end of the sentence, changing the text significantly, which is a disqualifying error in content accuracy. Additionally, Model 2's audio quality was static and did not follow the 'start smooth' instruction.
